title-group.article-title: A Stable Right-Sided Quaternion Fourier Regime for Multichannel Spectral Analysis

abstract.p: This paper defines a stable right-sided discrete quaternion Fourier regime for multichannel spectral analysis. It adopts a fixed transform axis i with forward transform X[k]=sum x[n]exp(-i2πnk/N) and inverse x[n]=(1/N)sum X[k]exp(+i2πnk/N).

Core results are exact inversion, right-linearity over the commuting slice C_i, spectral power nonnegativity, and Parseval invariance. The scope is intentionally limited to a one-dimensional fixed-axis right-sided seam for quaternion-lifted lanes.

license.license-p: This is an open-access article distributed under the terms of the Creative Commons Attribution License (CC BY 4.0).

sec.title: Introduction
sec.p: This manuscript selects one reproducible quaternion Fourier convention for downstream multichannel processing: one-dimensional index, right-sided kernel placement, and fixed axis i.

sec.title: Core results
sec.p: The paper proves right C_i-linearity, exact inversion, spectral power nonnegativity, and Parseval identity.
These invariants extend lanewise to grouped multichannel quaternion captures.

sec.title: Scope and limitations
sec.p: The manuscript does not claim universal superiority over other quaternion Fourier variants and does not address two-sided or higher-dimensional transforms.
